name: Grade 1 Spirit — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: Family-directed, non-devotional pathway
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Spirit pathway.

units.title: Investigate: family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to family-guided gratitude, wonder, traditions, and belonging.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
